title: Caprifolium hispidum
intro: Caprifolium hispidum, also known as the hairy honeysuckle or Caprifolium, is a flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as digestive issues and skin irritations. Bioactive constituents of Caprifolium hispidum include iridoids and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Caprifolium hispidum is a species of flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae. It is commonly known as the false honeysuckle. The plant is native to the southeastern United States. Caprifolium hispidum is a twining or climbing vine that is often found in woodland environments. It is classified as a member of the Caprifolieae tribe within the Caprifoliaceae family.
